description: A tender steak cut from the smaller end of the beef tenderloin. It is known for its soft texture and mild beef flavor and is commonly pan-seared or grilled and served as a premium steak dish.
preparation_steps: Sear in a hot pan or grill until browned.
Finish to the desired doneness, often with butter or herbs.
Serve with sauce or sides if desired.

health_advice.benefits: A concentrated source of protein and iron with relatively low carbohydrates.
health_advice.risks: Rich sauces and large portions can increase saturated fat and calories.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Keep portions moderate and pair with vegetables instead of only heavy sides.

dietary_notes.note: Naturally gluten-free when not served with flour-thickened sauces.

common_variations: Filet mignon with red wine sauce
